title: Digest-bind reviewed plan approval artifacts
goal: Bind reviewed-plan approval evidence to the exact immutable plan payload so post-review artifact changes are detectable without granting automatic approval or reviewer identity authority.

acceptance.assertion: Reviewed-plan evidence records a canonical digest of the exact effect-bearing plan payload and apply recomputes it before any effect.
Changes to actions, targets, expected output, source bindings or effect scope after review fail closed even when the current dry-run would otherwise permit the same operation.
The digest binding is represented in the reusable approval evidence contract rather than as an undocumented queue-only convention.
The implementation states and tests that a payload digest does not cryptographically authenticate reviewer identity unless a separate identity authority is provided.
Compatibility or migration behavior for existing reviewed-plan artifacts is explicit, and tests cover valid, missing, stale and tampered digests.

metadata.source_observation: Queue reconcile apply is now effect-safe through deterministic action parity plus root/head/queue binding, but reviewed_plan approval evidence still binds the artifact path rather than a digest of the exact reviewed payload.
metadata.safety_boundary: Digest binding may strengthen evidence integrity only; it must not auto-approve plans, infer reviewer identity, widen effect scope or bypass current state and post-effect gates.

lease_scope_migration.rationale: Digest-bound approval artifacts require policy code, artifact schemas and state-root evidence storage.
does_not_establish: safe mutation of terminal historical tasks
